fields.label: EasyEcom Tax Rule Name
fields.description: The opaque rule name EasyEcom carries on the product payload (e.g. 'GST', '5', 'tax_28%', 'TAx Rule5'). Never parsed — the FDE maps it to actual Item Tax Templates below.

fields.label: Company
fields.description: The Frappe Company this mapping is for. The natural key is (tax_rule_name, company) — one document per EE rule per Company; UNIQUE at the DB level.

fields.label: Workflow State
fields.description: Per §8.5.5: To Configure → Configured, branch Ignored. Owned by the Tax Rule Map Workflow fixture. New (rule, company) pairs auto-appear in To Configure when Item sync (8d) hits an unmapped one; the FDE may also pre-create them.

fields.label: Item Tax Templates
fields.description: ERPNext's native Item Tax child DocType — the same child used by the Item Taxes tab. One row for a flat rule; multiple banded rows for a slab rule (e.g. GST 5% [0–2500] + GST 18% [2500+]). The FDE selects real Item Tax Templates (e.g. 'GST 18% - {company_abbr}') and enters Min/Max Net Rate bands from EE's Tax Master UI. ERPNext resolves the band by net rate at invoice time — NO slab logic in our code (§8.5.2). The Configure workflow transition is gated on this table being non-empty.

fields.label: FDE Notes
fields.description: Free-form notes from the FDE about this mapping (e.g. 'GST slab confirmed against EE Tax Master screenshot 2026-05-25').
